question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCacheControlExpiryOrDefault()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `CACHE_CONTROL_REGEX = /^.*max-age=(\\d+)/` regex by influencing a response's `cache-control` header to contain a very large `max-age` value (e.g., `max-age=999999999`), causing cache entries to persist indefinitely in Redis and exhausting Redis memory over time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests to the same cached endpoint where `getCache().getSingleWithTtl(responseCacheKey)` is called, and if Redis is slow, cause many requests to await simultaneously, exhausting the Node.js async queue and causing memory pressure leading to DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: CachedApiResponse constructor] Can an unprivileged external user trigger caching of a response body that, when passed to `gzipSync(body)`, produces a compressed output that is larger than the input (e.g., already-compressed or random data), causing Redis to store unexpectedly large entries and exhausting Redis memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the spread operator `{...cachedResponse.headers, ...{[CACHE_CONTROL_HEADER]: ...}}` on a cached response with a very large `headers` object, causing excessive memory allocation during header merging on every cache hit and degrading performance under load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `responseCacheUpdateHandler` to be called with a `responseBody` that is a very large string, causing `JSONStringify` in `Cache.setSingle()` to serialize a massive object synchronously, blocking the event loop and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheKeyGenerator()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with URLs containing null bytes or special characters that cause `crypto.createHash('md5').update(req.originalUrl)` to behave unexpectedly, potentially generating colliding keys that overwrite legitimate cache entries and cause a functional DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `res.locals[requestStartTime]` falls back to `Date.now()` if not set, and by sending requests that manipulate timing, cause the elapsed time calculation to overflow or produce incorrect log entries that flood the logging system and cause I/O-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated cache updates for the same key by sending requests that always miss the cache (due to unique URL parameters), causing `Cache.setSingle()` to be called continuously and overwhelming Redis with write operations, degrading Redis performance for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: negotiate()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with `accept-encoding: gzip` to a cached compressed entry, causing `cachedResponse.getBody()` to allocate a new `Buffer.from(this.body, 'base64url')` on every request, and under high concurrency, exhaust Node.js heap memory through repeated large buffer allocations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Object.assign(new CachedApiResponse(), redisValue)` deserialization to inject a `body` field with an extremely large base64url-encoded string into Redis (via cache poisoning), causing subsequent `getBody()` or `getUncompressedBody()` calls to allocate massive buffers and exhaust memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition `if (responseBody && responseCacheKey && (isUnmodified || httpStatusCodes.isSuccess(res.statusCode)))` by triggering many successful responses with unique cache keys, causing unbounded growth of Redis memory as each unique URL gets its own cache entry?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: CachedApiResponse.getUncompressedBody()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a decompression bomb by causing a response with a small compressed body (high compression ratio) to be cached, then requesting it without gzip support, causing `unzipSync(Buffer.from(this.body, 'base64url'))` to expand the data massively and exhaust Node.js heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send many concurrent requests to endpoints with cached responses, causing many simultaneous `getCache().getSingleWithTtl()` Redis pipeline calls, and if Redis pipeline responses are slow, cause the Node.js async queue to fill with pending promises, exhausting memory and causing DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the header deletion logic (`delete headers[CACHE_CONTROL_HEADER]`, etc.) combined with a crafted response that has many headers, causing the header object manipulation to consume excessive CPU time under high concurrency and degrade service performance?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheKeyGenerator()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with URLs containing Unicode characters that expand significantly when processed by `crypto.createHash('md5').update(req.originalUrl)`, causing unexpected memory allocation and CPU usage that degrades performance under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `cachedResponse.headers` is spread into a new object on every cache hit (`{...cachedResponse.headers, ...}`), and by poisoning the cache with a response containing thousands of headers, cause excessive memory allocation on every subsequent request to that endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the `isUnmodified` (304) code path repeatedly by sending `if-none-match` requests that match cached ETags, causing `responseCacheUpdateHandler` to merge headers from multiple sources on every 304 response and store them back in Redis, amplifying Redis write load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: negotiate()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with `accept-encoding` headers that include quality values (e.g., `gzip;q=0.9, deflate;q=0.8, br;q=0.7`) requiring complex negotiation parsing by `new Negotiator(req)`, and under high concurrency, cause CPU saturation from repeated negotiation computations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `res.send(body)` call with a large cached body to cause the HTTP response serialization to consume excessive memory and CPU, and by sending many concurrent requests to the same large cached endpoint, cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCacheControlExpiryOrDefault()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `CACHE_CONTROL_REGEX` matching `parseInt(maxAge[1], 10)` to inject a `max-age` value of `0`, causing `ttl > 0` to be false and bypassing caching entirely for that endpoint, forcing all subsequent requests to hit the backend and causing a database DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a crafted `if-none-match` header that matches a cached ETag, triggering the 304 path where `delete headers[contentTypeHeader]` is called, and by doing this at high concurrency, cause excessive object mutation operations that degrade V8 hidden class optimization and slow down the entire process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `shouldCompress(responseBody)` to return `true` for many concurrent responses, causing multiple simultaneous `gzipSync(body)` calls (synchronous, blocking) to run concurrently in the same event loop tick, completely blocking the Node.js event loop and causing a DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: CachedApiResponse constructor] Can an unprivileged external user trigger caching of a response where `body.toString('base64url')` after `gzipSync` produces a string significantly larger than the original body, causing Redis to store entries that are larger than expected and exhausting Redis memory faster than anticipated?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on cache lookups to send thousands of requests per second to cached endpoints, causing `getCache().getSingleWithTtl()` to issue thousands of Redis pipeline commands per second, overwhelming Redis and causing it to become unresponsive for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: cacheKeyGenerator()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with URLs containing query parameters with very long values (e.g., `?param=AAAA...` with 100KB of data), causing `crypto.createHash('md5').update(req.originalUrl)` to process large inputs on every request and consume excessive CPU, leading to event-loop starvation?
